calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jan-13-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
PCN-17-300841, Conservatorship of Chun Ja Ellis
PTR-18-301772, In re: Chun Ja Ellis

Appearances:
Avid Eli Alavi for Verna Shaheen, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Counsel requested permission to sell an unimproved property in Tuolumne County and a 120-day listing agreement, citing the difficulty of finding buyers and agents for the property. The court granted permission for the sale and waived the requirement for court confirmation.

Discussion was held regarding the fourth accounting for both the conservatorship and the trust for a two-year period. The court noted that no questions or concerns were raised by the probate examiner regarding the transactions in the accounting. The court took the matters under submission with intent to grant.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi for and with Verna Shaheen (Trustee).

Ms. Alavi addressed the payment to Appel Tile stating that the house was in poor condition, that they had a Court order to make the renovations and that the repairs needed to be done. Ms. Alavi also stated that there were two payments at $3,566.00 and one at $3,568.00, that the amount of $10,700.00 was allowed in Court, that there was additional maintenance/work (base trim replacement) that needed to be done, that there were multiple runs, that there was 34 hours of work at $40.00 per hour for a total of $1,360.00 and that she has an invoice and is happy to file it. Ms. Shaheen stated that the work had to be done and that if they had gone with someone else, they would have had to pay more. In addition Ms. Alavi stated that when Buena Vista issues the invoice there is an optional gift that is divided amongst all of the staff, that the notice has a suggested amount of $300.00 which equates to less than $1.00 per day, spoke of the conservatorship having fees on account, stated that in the first accounting of November 2018 she did a foot note of two payments out of the trust, that it has already been deducted, that there limited funds in the conservatorship and that the significant funds have been transferred to/into the trust. Furthermore Ms. Alavi addressed the sale of the property stating that there were guns, that the guns had very little value, that they were taken to the police then taken to a dealer and were sold, that schedule F has a typo, that schedule E has the corrected investments and that she can provide a corrected schedule F today. The Court granted the First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. (subject to review of the order). Status on filing of the second account is set for October 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be in sync with the conservatorship accounting.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Authorize Repairs To Real Property; And Petition To Sell Personal Property
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for and with (Conservator) Verna Shaheen. Court Appointed Counsel Christopher Jew for (conservatee) Chun Ja Ellis.

The Court asked counsel to address the Examiner's notes. Ms. Alavi reported that she did not receive notes, that they would like to paint, replace windows and have termite work done, that they have 3 bids, that all of the work is necessary, that there are two rooms full of personal property that the conservatee intended to sell and the conservator would like to do so and that they need to decide if the conservatee will come back home or if the property will become a rental. Mr. Jew reported that the conservatee does not object to the repairs or to the sale of the personal property. The Court asked about the bid for flooring. Ms. Alavi stated that they have several bids and that Ms. Shaheen has worked previously with Greg Moore. The Court confirmed that there is only one bid for the termite work. Ms. Shaheen stated that the pest management company comes monthly for maintenance. The Court took the Petition to Authorize Repairs to Real Property and Petition to Sell Personal Property under submission. Ms. Alavi submitted a proposed order.

Judge:Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
